pr.title: Create union type for fighter x2070
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Factored the anonymous Fighter struct union at offset 0x2070 into a named `union Fighter2070` and updated call sites to access the fields through `fp->x2070.*`. The PR body says this was yoinked from PR #614 to make merging master into that PR easier. It was closed without merge, with no review comments in the provided slice.
what_changed: Introduced a named `union Fighter2070` in `fighter.h` containing the same byte, bitfield, and `s32 x2070_int` overlay previously embedded anonymously in `struct _Fighter` at offset 0x2070.
Replaced the anonymous inline union member in `struct _Fighter` with `/* 0x2070 */ union Fighter2070 x2070;`.

Updated fighter code and character special code to qualify prior direct accesses such as `fp->x2071_b6`, `fp->x2072_b4`, `fp->x2071_b0_3`, `fp->x2071_b5`, `fp->x2073`, and `fp->x2070_int` as `fp->x2070.x2071_b6`, `fp->x2070.x2072_b4`, `fp->x2070.x2071_b0_3`, `fp->x2070.x2071_b5`, `fp->x2070.x2073`, and `fp->x2070.x2070_int`.

Touched multiple fighter-special users of the 0x2070 field group, including Fox blaster checks, Luigi Green Missile setup, Mewtwo Shadow Ball removal checks, Pikachu state/property code, Samus state logic, action state changes, and shield-hit processing.
No semantic behavior change is evident from the diff; the changes are structural/type-access refactors intended to preserve the same layout and field meanings.
smart_moves: Named the offset-specific union instead of leaving it anonymous inside the large `Fighter` struct, making the 0x2070 overlay reusable and easier to refer to in future type work.
Kept the original offset-based field names inside the new union, preserving known decomp naming and minimizing churn while still improving type structure.

Preserved the `s32 x2070_int` overlay alongside byte and bitfield views, which matters because `Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC` uses a volatile read of the 32-bit value for stack/matching behavior.
Performed the refactor across all visible direct users so the codebase consistently treats the 0x2070 group as a subobject of `Fighter`.
The PR explicitly split work out from PR #614 to reduce merge friction, a useful strategy for landing isolated header/type cleanups before larger matching changes.
decomp_lessons: When a field group is both bitfield-addressed and whole-word-addressed, a named union can document the overlay while preserving matching-critical access patterns.

For large game structs like `Fighter`, extracting anonymous inline unions into named types can make future reverse engineering easier without changing layout, as long as all call sites are updated consistently.
Offset-derived names such as `Fighter2070` and `x2070` are acceptable transitional names when the real semantic purpose of a field group is not yet known.
Volatile dummy reads used for stack effects should be preserved exactly through refactors; here the access changed only from `fp->x2070_int` to `fp->x2070.x2070_int`.
Header-only layout refactors can have broad call-site impact across character-specific files, so grep-driven access updates are important.
naming_conventions: `union Fighter2070` names a reusable type after the owning struct and byte offset rather than guessing semantics.
The containing `Fighter` field is named `x2070`, matching its offset comment `/* 0x2070 */`.
The whole-word overlay keeps the name `x2070_int`, making explicit that the same 4 bytes can be accessed as an integer.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserved a matching-sensitive volatile local in `Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC`: `volatile s32 volatile_integer = fp->x2070.x2070_int;`.
Used a union to keep byte/bitfield access and 32-bit integer access over the same 0x2070 storage, avoiding layout changes that could alter generated code.
Updated member accesses without otherwise changing control flow, comparisons, constants, or function calls, which is the safest way to maintain existing matches during type cleanup.
The Samus expression was lightly reformatted while changing accesses, but the logic remained equivalent: checking `x2073 == 0x14` or `x2071_b5 == 0` through the new union field.
review_feedback: No line review comments or issue comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR was closed without merge shortly after creation; the provided evidence does not state why.
One review record exists in the counts, but no review body or actionable feedback is included in the excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Check PR #614 for the larger context that originally introduced or used `union Fighter2070`.
Search later history for `Fighter2070` to determine whether this closed PR's type extraction was landed in another PR.
Inspect current `fighter.h` around offset 0x2070 before reusing this pattern, since the PR itself was not merged.
Look for later renames of `x2071_b5`, `x2071_b6`, `x2072_b4`, or `x2073` to see whether their semantic meanings became known.
